response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a broad skillset, but he is missing the most critical hard requirements for this role. Most significantly, the position requires native-level Japanese language proficiency, and Bogdan lists only English, Romanian, and basic German — Japanese is entirely absent. Beyond that disqualifying gap, his profile shows no demonstrable experience in embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), LAN-switch firmware development, Linux kernel modification, or L2/L3 networking protocols. His C/C++ usage is limited to a few early roles and his bachelor thesis; his dominant stack for the past decade is TypeScript/Node.js/web frameworks, which does not align with the embedded C firmware focus of this position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and systems contexts demonstrates adaptability
Has worked with Linux environments and mentions embedded systems in his background summary and early roles
Early C/C++ work at National Instruments and audio systems thesis shows foundational systems programming exposure
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a stated hard requirement and alone disqualifies the candidate
Zero evidence of security protocol expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, MAC authentication, encryption key exchange) or embedded networking/switch product experience
Primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js/web stack for the last 5+ years, not C-based embedded firmware development
